title: The Aunt Who Came To Dinner

jokes.text: PIease, man. This ain't no restaurant. I mean, there wasn't even no goIden arches outside.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about Carlton's restaurant choice
jokes.punchline: McDonald's golden arches as his standard for what constitutes a restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic Will street-smart attitude contrasting with Carlton's upscale tastes. Good character work.

jokes.text: You're this far from washing windshieIds with newspaper.
jokes.setup: Carlton responding to Will's complaint about the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Equating Will's behavior to being homeless/desperate
jokes.explanation: Good comeback from Carlton showing his privileged perspective, well-structured putdown.

jokes.text: Now, just sit down and try to go a haIf-hour without saying "yo."
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to get Will to behave properly in the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Identifying Will's speech pattern as the specific problem
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific joke that perfectly captures Carlton's uptight nature vs Will's street vernacular.

jokes.text: Yo, yo, yo. I'II have two of those.
jokes.setup: Carlton just told him not to say 'yo' for thirty minutes
jokes.punchline: Will immediately says 'yo' three times in a row
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with escalation (three 'yo's). Classic sitcom setup/payoff timing.

jokes.text: Yo, CarIton, man, they French girIs. We in!
jokes.setup: Will spotting attractive French women
jokes.punchline: His excitement and assumption they have a chance
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's confidence and street attitude, but fairly standard horndog teen comedy.

jokes.text: Yo, they don't have no standards, man. They Iike Jerry Lewis.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why they have a chance with French women
jokes.punchline: Jerry Lewis's popularity in France as evidence of low standards
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference joke with good logic chain. Sharp and quotable.

jokes.text: This time I'II be the rap star and you be Gary CoIeman.
jokes.setup: Will assigning roles for their pickup scheme
jokes.punchline: Casting short Carlton as Gary Coleman
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor playing on Carlton's height and era-appropriate reference.

jokes.text: He's Tiny By Nature.
jokes.setup: French women asking what Will calls himself as a rap star
jokes.punchline: Carlton's rapper name playing on his small stature
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'Naughty By Nature' that works on multiple levels with Carlton's character.

jokes.text: A tennis court, swimming pooI, exceIIent schooI system.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing mansion amenities to impress French women
jokes.punchline: Including school system as a romantic selling point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his privileged, practical mindset making him accidentally hilarious.

jokes.text: Oh, no, man. Come on, they're famiIy. We bribe them.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggesting they lie to Hillary and Ashley
jokes.punchline: Will's moral correction that they should bribe family instead
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up moral stance then subverts to equally unethical but 'better' option.

jokes.text: Tomorrow we go French.
jokes.setup: Will summarizing their romantic plans
jokes.punchline: Setting up the contrast with paying for dinner
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next joke, works as anticipation builder.

jokes.text: Hey, CarIton, they're French girIs. They're aIways in the mood.
jokes.setup: Carlton planning to get them in the mood with Perrier
jokes.punchline: Will's stereotype about French women
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotype for humor, typical teen boy comedy but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Do you know the French word for forepIay?
jokes.setup: Will asking Carlton about French vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Setup for the punchline about no French word existing
jokes.explanation: Good setup that creates anticipation for the punchline.

jokes.text: Because there isn't one.
jokes.setup: French word for foreplay question
jokes.punchline: No French word exists because they don't need foreplay
jokes.explanation: Clean punchline delivery on a stereotype joke. Economical but relies on cliché.

jokes.text: But Iast time I squeaIed on HiIary... I found Barbie face down in the aquarium.
jokes.setup: Ashley agreeing to watch Hillary while parents are away
jokes.punchline: Hillary's violent retaliation involving drowning a Barbie
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures both Ashley's intelligence and Hillary's vindictiveness. Memorable image.

jokes.text: When I was young, Iyrics meant something. I was scatting, WiII.
jokes.setup: Phil defending his musical taste against Will's generation
jokes.punchline: Claiming scat singing had meaningful lyrics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's cultural pompousness, though the logic is questionable.

jokes.text: Has anybody seen my oId brown bag? / Excuse me. You used to caII me Diva.
jokes.setup: Phil asking for his old brown bag
jokes.punchline: Hillary correcting him that he used to call it 'Diva'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary moment - her vanity extends even to correcting what a purse is called.

jokes.text: So this is what a hernia feeIs Iike.
jokes.setup: Phil carrying heavy bags for Aunt Helen
jokes.punchline: His physical strain expressed through medical condition
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy reaction, gets the job done but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Buy a new hat, paint the house, get a divorce.
jokes.setup: Helen describing her spontaneous nature
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mundane activities to major life decisions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure that reveals character while building comedy through contrast.

jokes.text: Hey, does UncIe Lester stiII do that "puII my finger" thing in church?
jokes.setup: Serious adult conversation about marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Will's juvenile question about Uncle Lester's behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection - inappropriate timing but character-consistent teenage perspective.

jokes.text: That means it's about sex. Either UncIe Lester is seeing another woman or becoming one.
jokes.setup: Adults trying to keep conversation away from Ashley
jokes.punchline: Ashley's precocious analysis of the situation
jokes.explanation: Great Ashley character moment - too smart for her age with darkly funny conclusion.

jokes.text: Shoot! And it's transvestite week.
jokes.setup: Vivian saying no more Oprah
jokes.punchline: Ashley's disappointment about missing themed episodes
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Ashley's sophisticated media consumption and connects to her earlier joke.

jokes.text: In PhiIIy.
jokes.setup: Helen asking where she can stay, Will offering his room
jokes.punchline: His room is in Philadelphia, not helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy. Two-word punchline that completely reframes the offer.

jokes.text: You're staying at the Howard Johnson's, and I won't take no for an answer.
jokes.setup: Hillary appearing to be generous and hospitable
jokes.punchline: She's generously forcing Helen to stay at a hotel
jokes.explanation: Classic Hillary self-centeredness disguised as hospitality. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: You heard what she said. She'II be fine. Let's go, Vivian.
jokes.setup: Helen saying she'll be fine during her marital crisis
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately using it as excuse to leave
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Phil's desperation to avoid family drama.

jokes.text: I stayed up aII night Iearning the French for... "Forget about my cousin, y'aII can both have me."
jokes.setup: Carlton disappointed about their ruined date plans
jokes.punchline: Revealing his creepy prepared French pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton cringe - his earnest preparation for something completely inappropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, sweetie, that's aII right. You don't have to buy me anything. / I know. I meant for me.
jokes.setup: Hillary offering to buy something nice to cheer up Helen
jokes.punchline: Hillary clarifying she meant for herself
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Hillary moment - perfect character integration and misdirection structure.

jokes.text: GirI, I wouIdn't give Lester air if he was in a jug.
jokes.setup: Vivian suggesting Helen talk to Lester
jokes.punchline: Helen's colorful expression of how angry she is
jokes.explanation: Colorful vernacular expression that's both character-specific and memorably phrased.

jokes.text: You know how some men are animaIs in bed? / Yes. / WeII, I don't.
jokes.setup: Helen asking about men being animals in bed, Vivian agreeing
jokes.punchline: Helen revealing Lester isn't
jokes.explanation: Good structure and timing on intimate revelation. The pause sells it.

jokes.text: One day Iast month, I met PhiIip at the door... wearing nothing, honey, but Saran Wrap and a smiIe. WaIked right past me and said, "Hi, honey, did you make that meatIoaf?"
jokes.setup: Vivian relating to Helen's marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Phil's obliviousness to her romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy and character moment. The Saran Wrap detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: WeII, at Ieast he spoke.
jokes.setup: Vivian's story about Phil's obliviousness
jokes.punchline: Helen's dry comparison to her worse situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that acknowledges and tops Vivian's complaint.

jokes.text: I wouId have been reaching for my cute IittIe crowbar.
jokes.setup: Helen describing the cute little house of Lester's affair
jokes.punchline: Vivian's violent response maintaining the 'cute little' pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that maintains the pattern while subverting to violence. Shows Vivian's personality.

jokes.text: I couIdn't find it.
jokes.setup: Vivian's suggestion about using a crowbar
jokes.punchline: Helen's matter-of-fact response suggesting she looked for one
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that implies she seriously considered violence. Great timing.

jokes.text: You thought he said Aunt HeIen? No, no. He said Van HaIen.
jokes.setup: Will accidentally mentioning Aunt Helen on phone with French girls
jokes.punchline: Carlton's desperate cover story about the band Van Halen
jokes.explanation: Quick thinking cover-up that's believable enough while showing Carlton's panic.

jokes.text: Well, not to worry, my little chocolate truffles.
jokes.setup: Will trying to maintain the romantic facade on the phone
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top endearment for French girls
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Will's attempt at smooth talking, though the endearment is somewhat clichéd.

jokes.text: Prince and Hammer are coming by to pIay Yahtzee.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to make their house sound exciting to French women
jokes.punchline: Major rap stars playing the board game Yahtzee
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist humor that captures Carlton's square personality while being memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry, gotta go. Barry White's here.
jokes.setup: Carlton's increasingly ridiculous celebrity name-dropping
jokes.punchline: Adding Barry White to the fake celebrity gathering
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd escalation but with diminishing returns from the Yahtzee joke.

jokes.text: Not as much as we Iove French.... Toast.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying they love Bobby McFerrin
jokes.punchline: Will's awkward save by changing 'French women' to 'French toast'
jokes.explanation: Decent save attempt that creates awkwardness. The pause before 'toast' sells it.

jokes.text: Smith women tend to do that.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggesting Aunt Helen blew things out of proportion
jokes.punchline: Phil's generalization about his wife's family
jokes.explanation: Standard husband joke about in-laws. Gets a mild chuckle but nothing special.

jokes.text: Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Phil remembering his own Saran Wrap incident
jokes.punchline: His horrified realization
jokes.explanation: Good callback moment where Phil realizes his obliviousness. Physical reaction sells it.

jokes.text: Tomorrow on Geraldo, French women who can't say 'no.'
jokes.setup: Ashley watching TV during family drama
jokes.punchline: The show topic coincidentally relates to Will and Carlton's plans
jokes.explanation: Decent coincidental humor that connects to the main plot. Ashley's casual delivery while chaos happens around her is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: BeautifuI, sexy, and she measures everything in centimeters.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing why Monique is perfect
jokes.punchline: Including metric system as a romantic quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton joke - his nerdy obsessions extend to romantic preferences. Unique and character-specific.

jokes.text: the onIy sweet things we gonna be near is a coupIe KIondike bars.
jokes.setup: Will explaining they need to clear the house for their date
jokes.punchline: Alternative to sweet French women is ice cream bars
jokes.explanation: Decent metaphor but not particularly memorable. Gets the point across.

jokes.text: There is no more Saran Wrap and Baggies are out of the question.
jokes.setup: Phil calling Vivian urgently
jokes.punchline: His desperate attempt to recreate the romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Phil learned his lesson and is trying to fix things. Baggies detail is perfect.

jokes.text: I love you, Uncle Phil!
jokes.setup: Phil gets an extra jazz festival ticket so Helen can come
jokes.punchline: Will's enthusiasm is about getting the house cleared, not generosity
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Will's selfish relief. The enthusiasm contrasts with his usual attitude toward Phil's lectures.

jokes.text: I'II carry you there if I have to.
jokes.setup: Phil's enthusiasm about getting everyone to the jazz festival
jokes.punchline: His willingness to physically carry Helen
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup that plays on Phil's desperation to leave. Standard but effective.

jokes.text: Sir, I hope you brought your protective cup.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey announcing Lester's arrival to angry Helen
jokes.punchline: Warning about potential violence to Lester's groin
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey moment showing his dry wit and practical concerns.

jokes.text: Drag her home by her extensions?
jokes.setup: Lester saying he knows what he'll have to do if Helen won't leave
jokes.punchline: Will's assumption about dragging her by her hair extensions
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific detail about Helen's appearance and Will's street perspective.

jokes.text: Good oId Aunt Jemima. Now, that's a strong woman. I never saw her with a man.
jokes.setup: Will looking at syrup bottle during breakfast
jokes.punchline: Treating the mascot as a real person and feminist icon
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about breakfast iconography, though the cultural reference is problematic by today's standards.

jokes.text: ActuaIIy, I hear that she and the Quaker Oats dude... got kind of a JungIe Fever thing going.
jokes.setup: Will's comment about Aunt Jemima being single
jokes.punchline: Carlton creating interracial relationship between breakfast mascots
jokes.explanation: Extends the mascot joke but uses dated reference that's problematic. Carlton's delivery probably saves it.

jokes.text: Was it because I Ieft my wet underwear hanging on your wig stand?
jokes.setup: Lester trying to figure out why Helen left him
jokes.punchline: His obliviously inappropriate behavior with her hair accessories
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lester's cluelessness about boundaries and personal space.

jokes.text: Was it because I left my wet underwear hanging on your wig stand?
jokes.setup: Lester desperately guessing why Helen left
jokes.punchline: His specific, gross domestic habit
jokes.explanation: Great cringe humor with specific disgusting detail. Shows Lester's obliviousness and gross habits while building character.

jokes.text: The time I took that picture of you on the toiIet?
jokes.setup: Lester continuing to guess why Helen is angry
jokes.punchline: Escalating to even more inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Lester's complete lack of boundaries. Uncomfortable but funny.

jokes.text: That's it. I am never getting married.
jokes.setup: Adult conversation getting increasingly inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Ashley's mature decision based on evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ashley reaction - precocious kid making logical decisions based on adult dysfunction.

jokes.text: HiIary, wouId you pIease ask the aduIterer on your right to pass the biscuits?
jokes.setup: Tense family dinner after fight
jokes.punchline: Helen using Hillary as intermediary with insulting description
jokes.explanation: Classic passive-aggressive dinner table behavior. The formality makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No, not Lester the schizophrenic, Lester the impotent guy.
jokes.setup: Helen calling doctor's office about Lester
jokes.punchline: Distinguishing between multiple Lesters by their conditions
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mental health and medical issues. Shows Helen's blunt honesty.

jokes.text: but there's part of me that wouId prefer a man.
jokes.setup: Lester talking to his therapist on phone
jokes.punchline: Comment that sounds like sexual preference but refers to therapy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays with audience assumptions about therapy and sexuality.

jokes.text: WouId you reaIIy prefer a man?
jokes.setup: Will overhearing Lester's therapy conversation
jokes.punchline: Will's genuine curiosity about what he thinks he heard
jokes.explanation: Natural follow-up that shows Will's teenage directness and curiosity.

jokes.text: your passive-aggressive feeIings towards your absent father
jokes.setup: Will getting angry at Lester for cheating
jokes.punchline: Lester psychoanalyzing Will's motivations using therapy speak
jokes.explanation: Meta moment that references Will's backstory while showing Lester's therapy influence.

jokes.text: Man, you reaIIy must be seeing a shrink.
jokes.setup: Lester's psychological analysis
jokes.punchline: Will's impressed/annoyed reaction to the accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that acknowledges the hit while deflecting with humor.

jokes.text: what does it mean when you dream... that you're fIying through a tunneI on a Scud missiIe?
jokes.setup: Will getting free therapy advice
jokes.punchline: His bizarre, topical dream involving Gulf War missiles
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist dream with era-specific reference. Shows Will's subconscious concerns.

jokes.text: I'm the one who signed his name to aII your damn birthday cards.
jokes.setup: Helen defending Will from Lester
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's been the caring family member all along
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that explains family dynamics while being funny.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Aunt HeIen. My shower's broken. / UncIe PhiI fixed it.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why he's in bathroom with Helen
jokes.punchline: Phil's convenient timing fixing the shower
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with convenient timing for the plot complication.

jokes.text: "I was trapped in a bathroom with my naked aunt."
jokes.setup: Will imagining explaining his missed date
jokes.punchline: The impossibly embarrassing but accurate explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - the truth is more embarrassing than any lie could be.

jokes.text: I bet the hussy has a weave.
jokes.setup: Helen talking about Lester's supposed mistress
jokes.punchline: Her specific assumption about the other woman's hair
jokes.explanation: Character-specific jealousy detail that shows Helen's priorities and insecurities.

jokes.text: If the rooster ain't crowing in the henhouse... he must be cock-a-doodIing in the barn.
jokes.setup: Helen explaining her suspicions about Lester
jokes.punchline: Colorful barnyard metaphor for infidelity
jokes.explanation: Good vernacular expression that's both colorful and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: When that job went south, weII, my doodIe went with it.
jokes.setup: Lester explaining his confidence issues after job loss
jokes.punchline: Using Helen's barnyard metaphor for his sexual problems
jokes.explanation: Good callback that uses Helen's metaphor for intimate revelation. Awkward but effective.

jokes.text: They doing it.
jokes.setup: Sounds from Helen and Lester's reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that acknowledges the obvious. Economy of words makes it work.

jokes.text: No. Because we're moving and we're not gonna teII you where.
jokes.setup: Helen saying this won't happen again because they love each other
jokes.punchline: Phil's practical solution to avoid future drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil moment - practical, blunt, and selfish but understandable. Great misdirection structure.

jokes.text: I don't think it was the Fourth of JuIy, but I do remember fireworks.
jokes.setup: Phil asking about their last skinny-dip in the Jacuzzi
jokes.punchline: Vivian's innuendo about their sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Clean innuendo that works on multiple levels. Good structure and delivery.

jokes.text: But, Monique, we do not have the bathing suits.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting they use the Jacuzzi
jokes.punchline: French woman's practical concern about swimwear
jokes.explanation: Setup for the punchline but not particularly strong on its own.

jokes.text: No probIem, neither do we.
jokes.setup: French women concerned about not having bathing suits
jokes.punchline: Will's smooth suggestion of skinny dipping
jokes.explanation: Clean punchline with good economy. Sets up the final complication.

jokes.text: WiII! / Father! / CarIton! / Mother!
jokes.setup: Everyone unexpectedly encountering each other in various states of undress
jokes.punchline: Chain reaction of shocked recognition
jokes.explanation: Classic farce moment with escalating recognition. Physical comedy that builds to chaos.

jokes.text: Don't make me have to get ethnic on you! / I'm just so excited.
jokes.setup: Chaos in the house during the encounter
jokes.punchline: Helen's repeated threat mixed with Hillary's oblivious enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Callback during chaos that shows character consistency in crisis.

jokes.text: You're staying at the Howard Johnson.
jokes.setup: All the chaos and embarrassment of the episode
jokes.punchline: Will's final word bringing back Hillary's hotel suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending that ties the episode together. Hillary's selfish solution becomes the sensible one.
